[The claim that "One can crack their teeth biting a Pecan" is a common saying, but it is not accurate. Pecans are hard nuts, and while they are strong, they are not designed to break teeth. The idea that biting a pecan could crack teeth is a misconception. It is not common sense to assume that such a scenario is possible. The claim is not supported by human common sense.]

*** 

[The claim is based on a misunderstanding of the physical properties of pecans. Pecans are hard and have a tough outer shell, but they are not strong enough to cause significant damage to teeth. The idea that a pecan can crack teeth is not supported by scientific evidence or common knowledge. It is not a widely accepted fact, and it is not something that can be verified by human common sense alone. The claim is not correct.]
